# protobuf


---

### FileDescriptorSet

The protocol compiler can output a FileDescriptorSet containing the .proto
files it parses.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>file</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>FileDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### FileDescriptorProto

Describes a complete .proto file.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>file name, relative to root of source tree</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>package</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>e.g. "foo", "foo.bar", etc.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>dependency</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>string[]</code><br/><br/>Names of files imported by this file.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>public_dependency</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>int32[]</code><br/><br/>Indexes of the public imported files in the dependency list above.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>weak_dependency</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>int32[]</code><br/><br/>Indexes of the weak imported files in the dependency list.
For Google-internal migration only. Do not use.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>option_dependency</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>string[]</code><br/><br/>Names of files imported by this file purely for the purpose of providing
option extensions. These are excluded from the dependency list above.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>message_type</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>DescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/>All top-level definitions in this file.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>enum_type</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>EnumDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>service</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>ServiceDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>extension</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>FieldDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FileOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>source_code_info</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>SourceCodeInfo</code><br/><br/>This field contains optional information about the original source code.
You may safely remove this entire field without harming runtime
functionality of the descriptors -- the information is needed only by
development tools.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>syntax</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>The syntax of the proto file.
The supported values are "proto2", "proto3", and "editions".

If `edition` is present, this value must be "editions".
WARNING: This field should only be used by protobuf plugins or special
cases like the proto compiler. Other uses are discouraged and
developers should rely on the protoreflect APIs for their client language.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>edition</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>Edition</code><br/><br/>The edition of the proto file.
WARNING: This field should only be used by protobuf plugins or special
cases like the proto compiler. Other uses are discouraged and
developers should rely on the protoreflect APIs for their client language.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### DescriptorProto

Describes a message type.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>field</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>FieldDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>extension</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>FieldDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>nested_type</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>DescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>enum_type</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>EnumDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>extension_range</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>ExtensionRange[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>oneof_decl</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>OneofDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>MessageOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>reserved_range</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>ReservedRange[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>reserved_name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>string[]</code><br/><br/>Reserved field names, which may not be used by fields in the same message.
A given name may only be reserved once.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>visibility</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>SymbolVisibility</code><br/><br/>Support for `export` and `local` keywords on enums.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### ExtensionRangeOptions

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>uninterpreted_option</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>UninterpretedOption[]</code><br/><br/>The parser stores options it doesn't recognize here. See above.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>declaration</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>Declaration[]</code><br/><br/>For external users: DO NOT USE. We are in the process of open sourcing
extension declaration and executing internal cleanups before it can be
used externally.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>features</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FeatureSet</code><br/><br/>Any features defined in the specific edition.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>verification</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>VerificationState</code><br/><br/>The verification state of the range.
TODO: flip the default to DECLARATION once all empty ranges
are marked as UNVERIFIED.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### FieldDescriptorProto

Describes a field within a message.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>number</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>int32</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>label</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>Label</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>type</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>Type</code><br/><br/>If type_name is set, this need not be set.  If both this and type_name
are set, this must be one of TYPE_ENUM, TYPE_MESSAGE or TYPE_GROUP.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>type_name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>For message and enum types, this is the name of the type.  If the name
starts with a '.', it is fully-qualified.  Otherwise, C++-like scoping
rules are used to find the type (i.e. first the nested types within this
message are searched, then within the parent, on up to the root
namespace).</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>extendee</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>For extensions, this is the name of the type being extended.  It is
resolved in the same manner as type_name.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>default_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>For numeric types, contains the original text representation of the value.
For booleans, "true" or "false".
For strings, contains the default text contents (not escaped in any way).
For bytes, contains the C escaped value.  All bytes >= 128 are escaped.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>oneof_index</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>int32</code><br/><br/>If set, gives the index of a oneof in the containing type's oneof_decl
list.  This field is a member of that oneof.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>json_name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>JSON name of this field. The value is set by protocol compiler. If the
user has set a "json_name" option on this field, that option's value
will be used. Otherwise, it's deduced from the field's name by converting
it to camelCase.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FieldOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>proto3_optional</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>If true, this is a proto3 "optional". When a proto3 field is optional, it
tracks presence regardless of field type.

When proto3_optional is true, this field must belong to a oneof to signal
to old proto3 clients that presence is tracked for this field. This oneof
is known as a "synthetic" oneof, and this field must be its sole member
(each proto3 optional field gets its own synthetic oneof). Synthetic oneofs
exist in the descriptor only, and do not generate any API. Synthetic oneofs
must be ordered after all "real" oneofs.

For message fields, proto3_optional doesn't create any semantic change,
since non-repeated message fields always track presence. However it still
indicates the semantic detail of whether the user wrote "optional" or not.
This can be useful for round-tripping the .proto file. For consistency we
give message fields a synthetic oneof also, even though it is not required
to track presence. This is especially important because the parser can't
tell if a field is a message or an enum, so it must always create a
synthetic oneof.

Proto2 optional fields do not set this flag, because they already indicate
optional with `LABEL_OPTIONAL`.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### OneofDescriptorProto

Describes a oneof.

<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>

<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>OneofOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### EnumDescriptorProto

Describes an enum type.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>EnumValueDescriptorProto[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>EnumOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>reserved_range</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>EnumReservedRange[]</code><br/><br/>Range of reserved numeric values. Reserved numeric values may not be used
by enum values in the same enum declaration. Reserved ranges may not
overlap.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>reserved_name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>string[]</code><br/><br/>Reserved enum value names, which may not be reused. A given name may only
be reserved once.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>visibility</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>SymbolVisibility</code><br/><br/>Support for `export` and `local` keywords on enums.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>number</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>int32</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>EnumValueOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>

### MethodDescriptorProto

Describes a method of a service.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>

</tr></thead>

<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>

<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>

FieldDescriptorProto.type_name, but must refer to a message type.</td>
</tr>

</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>options</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>MethodOptions</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>client_streaming</code></td>

</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>server_streaming</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Identifies if server streams multiple server messages</td>
</tr>

### FileOptions

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>java_package</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Sets the Java package where classes generated from this .proto will be
placed.  By default, the proto package is used, but this is often
inappropriate because proto packages do not normally start with backwards
domain names.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>java_outer_classname</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Controls the name of the wrapper Java class generated for the .proto file.
That class will always contain the .proto file's getDescriptor() method as
well as any top-level extensions defined in the .proto file.
If java_multiple_files is disabled, then all the other classes from the
.proto file will be nested inside the single wrapper outer class.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>java_multiple_files</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>If enabled, then the Java code generator will generate a separate .java
file for each top-level message, enum, and service defined in the .proto
file.  Thus, these types will *not* be nested inside the wrapper class
named by java_outer_classname.  However, the wrapper class will still be
generated to contain the file's getDescriptor() method as well as any
top-level extensions defined in the file.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>java_generate_equals_and_hash</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>This option does nothing.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>java_string_check_utf8</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>A proto2 file can set this to true to opt in to UTF-8 checking for Java,
which will throw an exception if invalid UTF-8 is parsed from the wire or
assigned to a string field.

TODO: clarify exactly what kinds of field types this option
applies to, and update these docs accordingly.

Proto3 files already perform these checks. Setting the option explicitly to
false has no effect: it cannot be used to opt proto3 files out of UTF-8
checks.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>optimize_for</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>OptimizeMode</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>go_package</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Sets the Go package where structs generated from this .proto will be
placed. If omitted, the Go package will be derived from the following:
  - The basename of the package import path, if provided.
  - Otherwise, the package statement in the .proto file, if present.
  - Otherwise, the basename of the .proto file, without extension.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>cc_generic_services</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Should generic services be generated in each language?  "Generic" services
are not specific to any particular RPC system.  They are generated by the
main code generators in each language (without additional plugins).
Generic services were the only kind of service generation supported by
early versions of google.protobuf.

Generic services are now considered deprecated in favor of using plugins
that generate code specific to your particular RPC system.  Therefore,
these default to false.  Old code which depends on generic services should
explicitly set them to true.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>java_generic_services</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>py_generic_services</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>deprecated</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Is this file deprecated?
Depending on the target platform, this can emit Deprecated annotations
for everything in the file, or it will be completely ignored; in the very
least, this is a formalization for deprecating files.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>cc_enable_arenas</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Enables the use of arenas for the proto messages in this file. This applies
only to generated classes for C++.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>objc_class_prefix</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Sets the objective c class prefix which is prepended to all objective c
generated classes from this .proto. There is no default.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>csharp_namespace</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Namespace for generated classes; defaults to the package.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>swift_prefix</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>By default Swift generators will take the proto package and CamelCase it
replacing '.' with underscore and use that to prefix the types/symbols
defined. When this options is provided, they will use this value instead
to prefix the types/symbols defined.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>php_class_prefix</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Sets the php class prefix which is prepended to all php generated classes
from this .proto. Default is empty.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>php_namespace</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Use this option to change the namespace of php generated classes. Default
is empty. When this option is empty, the package name will be used for
determining the namespace.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>php_metadata_namespace</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Use this option to change the namespace of php generated metadata classes.
Default is empty. When this option is empty, the proto file name will be
used for determining the namespace.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>ruby_package</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>Use this option to change the package of ruby generated classes. Default
is empty. When this option is not set, the package name will be used for
determining the ruby package.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>features</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FeatureSet</code><br/><br/>Any features defined in the specific edition.
WARNING: This field should only be used by protobuf plugins or special
cases like the proto compiler. Other uses are discouraged and
developers should rely on the protoreflect APIs for their client language.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>uninterpreted_option</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>UninterpretedOption[]</code><br/><br/>The parser stores options it doesn't recognize here.
See the documentation for the "Options" section above.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### MessageOptions

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>message_set_wire_format</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Set true to use the old proto1 MessageSet wire format for extensions.
This is provided for backwards-compatibility with the MessageSet wire
format.  You should not use this for any other reason:  It's less
efficient, has fewer features, and is more complicated.

The message must be defined exactly as follows:
  message Foo {
    option message_set_wire_format = true;
    extensions 4 to max;
  }
Note that the message cannot have any defined fields; MessageSets only
have extensions.

All extensions of your type must be singular messages; e.g. they cannot
be int32s, enums, or repeated messages.

Because this is an option, the above two restrictions are not enforced by
the protocol compiler.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>no_standard_descriptor_accessor</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Disables the generation of the standard "descriptor()" accessor, which can
conflict with a field of the same name.  This is meant to make migration
from proto1 easier; new code should avoid fields named "descriptor".</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>deprecated</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Is this message deprecated?
Depending on the target platform, this can emit Deprecated annotations
for the message, or it will be completely ignored; in the very least,
this is a formalization for deprecating messages.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>map_entry</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Whether the message is an automatically generated map entry type for the
maps field.

For maps fields:
    map<KeyType, ValueType> map_field = 1;
The parsed descriptor looks like:
    message MapFieldEntry {
        option map_entry = true;
        optional KeyType key = 1;
        optional ValueType value = 2;
    }
    repeated MapFieldEntry map_field = 1;

Implementations may choose not to generate the map_entry=true message, but
use a native map in the target language to hold the keys and values.
The reflection APIs in such implementations still need to work as
if the field is a repeated message field.

NOTE: Do not set the option in .proto files. Always use the maps syntax
instead. The option should only be implicitly set by the proto compiler
parser.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>deprecated_legacy_json_field_conflicts</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Enable the legacy handling of JSON field name conflicts.  This lowercases
and strips underscored from the fields before comparison in proto3 only.
The new behavior takes `json_name` into account and applies to proto2 as
well.

This should only be used as a temporary measure against broken builds due
to the change in behavior for JSON field name conflicts.

TODO This is legacy behavior we plan to remove once downstream
teams have had time to migrate.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>features</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FeatureSet</code><br/><br/>Any features defined in the specific edition.
WARNING: This field should only be used by protobuf plugins or special
cases like the proto compiler. Other uses are discouraged and
developers should rely on the protoreflect APIs for their client language.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>uninterpreted_option</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>UninterpretedOption[]</code><br/><br/>The parser stores options it doesn't recognize here. See above.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### FieldOptions

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>ctype</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>CType</code><br/><br/>NOTE: ctype is deprecated. Use `features.(pb.cpp).string_type` instead.
The ctype option instructs the C++ code generator to use a different
representation of the field than it normally would.  See the specific
options below.  This option is only implemented to support use of
[ctype=CORD] and [ctype=STRING] (the default) on non-repeated fields of
type "bytes" in the open source release.
TODO: make ctype actually deprecated.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>packed</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>The packed option can be enabled for repeated primitive fields to enable
a more efficient representation on the wire. Rather than repeatedly
writing the tag and type for each element, the entire array is encoded as
a single length-delimited blob. In proto3, only explicit setting it to
false will avoid using packed encoding.  This option is prohibited in
Editions, but the `repeated_field_encoding` feature can be used to control
the behavior.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>jstype</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>JSType</code><br/><br/>The jstype option determines the JavaScript type used for values of the
field.  The option is permitted only for 64 bit integral and fixed types
(int64, uint64, sint64, fixed64, sfixed64).  A field with jstype JS_STRING
is represented as JavaScript string, which avoids loss of precision that
can happen when a large value is converted to a floating point JavaScript.
Specifying JS_NUMBER for the jstype causes the generated JavaScript code to
use the JavaScript "number" type.  The behavior of the default option
JS_NORMAL is implementation dependent.

This option is an enum to permit additional types to be added, e.g.
goog.math.Integer.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>lazy</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Should this field be parsed lazily?  Lazy applies only to message-type
fields.  It means that when the outer message is initially parsed, the
inner message's contents will not be parsed but instead stored in encoded
form.  The inner message will actually be parsed when it is first accessed.

This is only a hint.  Implementations are free to choose whether to use
eager or lazy parsing regardless of the value of this option.  However,
setting this option true suggests that the protocol author believes that
using lazy parsing on this field is worth the additional bookkeeping
overhead typically needed to implement it.

This option does not affect the public interface of any generated code;
all method signatures remain the same.  Furthermore, thread-safety of the
interface is not affected by this option; const methods remain safe to
call from multiple threads concurrently, while non-const methods continue
to require exclusive access.

Note that lazy message fields are still eagerly verified to check
ill-formed wireformat or missing required fields. Calling IsInitialized()
on the outer message would fail if the inner message has missing required
fields. Failed verification would result in parsing failure (except when
uninitialized messages are acceptable).</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>unverified_lazy</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>unverified_lazy does no correctness checks on the byte stream. This should
only be used where lazy with verification is prohibitive for performance
reasons.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>deprecated</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Is this field deprecated?
Depending on the target platform, this can emit Deprecated annotations
for accessors, or it will be completely ignored; in the very least, this
is a formalization for deprecating fields.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>weak</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>DEPRECATED. DO NOT USE!
For Google-internal migration only. Do not use.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>debug_redact</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bool</code><br/><br/>Indicate that the field value should not be printed out when using debug
formats, e.g. when the field contains sensitive credentials.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>retention</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>OptionRetention</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>targets</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>OptionTargetType[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>edition_defaults</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>EditionDefault[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>features</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FeatureSet</code><br/><br/>Any features defined in the specific edition.
WARNING: This field should only be used by protobuf plugins or special
cases like the proto compiler. Other uses are discouraged and
developers should rely on the protoreflect APIs for their client language.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>feature_support</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>FeatureSupport</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>uninterpreted_option</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>UninterpretedOption[]</code><br/><br/>The parser stores options it doesn't recognize here. See above.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### UninterpretedOption

A message representing a option the parser does not recognize. This only
appears in options protos created by the compiler::Parser class.
DescriptorPool resolves these when building Descriptor objects. Therefore,
options protos in descriptor objects (e.g. returned by Descriptor::options(),
or produced by Descriptor::CopyTo()) will never have UninterpretedOptions
in them.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>name</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>NamePart[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>identifier_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/>The value of the uninterpreted option, in whatever type the tokenizer
identified it as during parsing. Exactly one of these should be set.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>positive_int_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>uint64</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>negative_int_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>int64</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>double_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>double</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>string_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>bytes</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>aggregate_value</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>string</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### FeatureSet

TODO Enums in C++ gencode (and potentially other languages) are
not well scoped.  This means that each of the feature enums below can clash
with each other.  The short names we've chosen maximize call-site
readability, but leave us very open to this scenario.  A future feature will
be designed and implemented to handle this, hopefully before we ever hit a
conflict here.

### FeatureSetDefaults

A compiled specification for the defaults of a set of features.  These
messages are generated from FeatureSet extensions and can be used to seed
feature resolution. The resolution with this object becomes a simple search
for the closest matching edition, followed by proto merges.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>defaults</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>FeatureSetEditionDefault[]</code><br/><br/></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>minimum_edition</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>Edition</code><br/><br/>The minimum supported edition (inclusive) when this was constructed.
Editions before this will not have defaults.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>maximum_edition</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><span style={{fontSize:'0.75rem',background:'#059669',color:'white',padding:'2px 8px',borderRadius:'4px',marginRight:'0.5rem'}}>optional</span> <code>Edition</code><br/><br/>The maximum known edition (inclusive) when this was constructed. Editions
after this will not have reliable defaults.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### SourceCodeInfo

Encapsulates information about the original source file from which a
FileDescriptorProto was generated.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>location</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>Location[]</code><br/><br/>A Location identifies a piece of source code in a .proto file which
corresponds to a particular definition.  This information is intended
to be useful to IDEs, code indexers, documentation generators, and similar
tools.

For example, say we have a file like:
  message Foo {
    optional string foo = 1;
  }
Let's look at just the field definition:
  optional string foo = 1;
  ^       ^^     ^^  ^  ^^^
  a       bc     de  f  ghi
We have the following locations:
  span   path               represents
  [a,i)  [ 4, 0, 2, 0 ]     The whole field definition.
  [a,b)  [ 4, 0, 2, 0, 4 ]  The label (optional).
  [c,d)  [ 4, 0, 2, 0, 5 ]  The type (string).
  [e,f)  [ 4, 0, 2, 0, 1 ]  The name (foo).
  [g,h)  [ 4, 0, 2, 0, 3 ]  The number (1).

Notes:
- A location may refer to a repeated field itself (i.e. not to any
  particular index within it).  This is used whenever a set of elements are
  logically enclosed in a single code segment.  For example, an entire
  extend block (possibly containing multiple extension definitions) will
  have an outer location whose path refers to the "extensions" repeated
  field without an index.
- Multiple locations may have the same path.  This happens when a single
  logical declaration is spread out across multiple places.  The most
  obvious example is the "extend" block again -- there may be multiple
  extend blocks in the same scope, each of which will have the same path.
- A location's span is not always a subset of its parent's span.  For
  example, the "extendee" of an extension declaration appears at the
  beginning of the "extend" block and is shared by all extensions within
  the block.
- Just because a location's span is a subset of some other location's span
  does not mean that it is a descendant.  For example, a "group" defines
  both a type and a field in a single declaration.  Thus, the locations
  corresponding to the type and field and their components will overlap.
- Code which tries to interpret locations should probably be designed to
  ignore those that it doesn't understand, as more types of locations could
  be recorded in the future.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### GeneratedCodeInfo

Describes the relationship between generated code and its original source
file. A GeneratedCodeInfo message is associated with only one generated
source file, but may contain references to different source .proto files.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Name</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}></th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>annotation</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}><code>Annotation[]</code><br/><br/>An Annotation connects some span of text in generated code to an element
of its generating .proto file.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### Edition

The full set of known editions.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Value</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}>Description</th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_UNKNOWN</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>A placeholder for an unknown edition value.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_LEGACY</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>A placeholder edition for specifying default behaviors *before* a feature
was first introduced.  This is effectively an "infinite past".</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_PROTO2</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>Legacy syntax "editions".  These pre-date editions, but behave much like
distinct editions.  These can't be used to specify the edition of proto
files, but feature definitions must supply proto2/proto3 defaults for
backwards compatibility.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_PROTO3</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_2023</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>Editions that have been released.  The specific values are arbitrary and
should not be depended on, but they will always be time-ordered for easy
comparison.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_2024</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_UNSTABLE</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>A placeholder edition for developing and testing unscheduled features.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_1_TEST_ONLY</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>Placeholder editions for testing feature resolution.  These should not be
used or relied on outside of tests.</td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_2_TEST_ONLY</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_99997_TEST_ONLY</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_99998_TEST_ONLY</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_99999_TEST_ONLY</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>EDITION_MAX</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}>Placeholder for specifying unbounded edition support.  This should only
ever be used by plugins that can expect to never require any changes to
support a new edition.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

---

### SymbolVisibility

Describes the 'visibility' of a symbol with respect to the proto import
system. Symbols can only be imported when the visibility rules do not prevent
it (ex: local symbols cannot be imported).  Visibility modifiers can only set
on `message` and `enum` as they are the only types available to be referenced
from other files.

<table style={{width:'100%',borderCollapse:'collapse'}}>
<thead><tr style={{borderBottom:'2px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.3)'}}>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600,width:'30%'}}>Value</th>
<th style={{textAlign:'left',padding:'8px 12px',fontWeight:600}}>Description</th>
</tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>VISIBILITY_UNSET</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>VISIBILITY_LOCAL</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
<tr style={{borderBottom:'1px solid rgba(125,125,125,0.15)'}}>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px',verticalAlign:'top',whiteSpace:'nowrap'}}><code>VISIBILITY_EXPORT</code></td>
<td style={{padding:'8px 12px'}}></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
